scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Inside her house, Regina is putting on her earrings in front of a mirror when she hears the doorbell ring. She opens the door to find nothing but a basket filled with green apples.

dialog.dialog: A gift. From sister to sister.
What are you doing here?
I saw your tree and I thought you could use something better.  Red apples are so sickly sweet, don't you think? People tend to like something... a little sharper.
And green apples are just... bitter. . That's not yours.
Story of my life.
Why are you here?
Oh, sisterly concerns, sisterly advice, you know. I wanted to make sure you were all right after our little showdown.
I'm fine. Are you?
Getting there.  This is nice. So luxurious. You really don't appreciate what you have, do you?
Right. Because I got everything and you have nothing. If you want your superpower to be envy, go right ahead.
"Envy" is just another word for "ambition".
Well, that's just  not true.

You see, I strive for things. I  —you cast a curse that was just a fancy form of running away. With every advantage, you still turn your back on every opportunity for happiness. You, Regina— you just don't take risks, you don't live your life! It's such a waste!  I can see why our mother was so disappointed in you.
Disappointed in me? She gave you away.
You have no idea what really happened. That's exactly what's going to hurt you.
You didn't come here to give me "sisterly advice". Why are you really here?
So perceptive. I'm here, Regina, because I wanted to make sure you weren't someplace else so I can take what I need. Or rather, the Dark One could. Do you feel it, sis? He's taking your heart.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. In the the Merry Men's camp in the woods near the edge of town, Mr. Gold is standing in front of Robin Hood and the Merry Men, all with their weapons ready.

dialog.dialog: Stop right there. You know this arrow never misses its mark.
If I could stand down, I would. Heed me. Give me the heart.  And who is this?
Roland! Go back!
Come here, little boy.
Don't! Please!
I don't want to. I really don't. Come closer, little boy.
Daddy!
And stop.
Daddy! Help!
I didn't want to do this either.
It never misses its target. And I've just changed the target. If I drop my finger...
No!  Wait! Wait.
Thank you. And I am sorry.
It's okay. It's okay, boy. You're safe.  Go to Friar Tuck
What happened?
I'm sorry. It's gone.
Was anyone hurt?
Luckily, no. Again, I must apologize. You trusted me, and I let you down.
No, you didn't. Nothing's worth the loss of a child. But now we have a problem. I'm alive.
I don't follow.
She has my heart. Gold's taken it for her, and since she hasn't crushed it and killed me, that means she needs it for something far worse.
Worse than murder? What is she planning?
It doesn't matter. Because I'm going to stop her.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Inside Mr. Gold Pawnbroker & Antiquities Dealer, Regina is rummaging through the items, looking for something to help her with the situation.

dialog.dialog: This is all useless crap!
Hey! Hey, hey! Stop! Stop it! What—what are you looking for?

I need to destroy my sister. She has my heart.  Oh, relax. She can't control me with it. I protected it from that. The problem is, I don't know what she's planning on using it for, which is why I need to take her out!  That's why I need your help. What do you have here?

"What do I have here"? Self-respect. Why on Earth or any realm would I help you? The woman who imprisoned me in a tower in her castle, then put me in an asylum for twenty-eight years, who's done nothing but mentally and physically torture me ever since we've known each other!
Huh. Bookworm's got teeth.
Get out.

Yes. I did all those horrible things... in the past. But right now, I need something to defeat the woman who's puppet-mastering your boyfriend. She has your Rumple, and unless you help me, you may never see him again. I'm sorry, Belle. I really, really am.

So, I've been trying to match the ingredients that Zelena's been collecting to whatever spell she intends to cast. It would really help if I knew what it so special about your heart. Does she just want it because you're sisters, or...
I have no idea.  The candle. Where's the candle?
Here?
Not that that, Liberace. The two-sided candle Snow used to kill my mother. Where's that?
Its power's gone now. It's just a relic.
Yes. That's it. This isn't about how my mother died. This is about how she lived.

scenes.sceneDesc: Enchanted Forest. Past. In a tavern, a young Cora works as a bar maid. A man hands her a tray containing multiple mugs of beer.

dialog.dialog: Here you go, Cora.
Cora! There she is!
Here you go.  Oh! Off with you!
Give us a dance,lassie!
Oh, let go, Charlie.  Oh, I'm so sorry.
Sit down, Cora. You've already worked past the end of your shift. I daresay you've earned it.
Thank you.
You know, I've watched you this whole week that I've been here. You work in a tavern, but you move like a lady.
I'm just a miller's daughter. I earn a little extra here. Someone once told me to act like what you plan to be, so I try to be a little better than what I am.
Oh, I wish I could stay. But tomorrow, I must press on. I've already stayed longer than I planned.
Have you? Why?
I think you know why.  Oh, that's—I didn't want you to...
The royal crest.
I wanted you to know me as me, not as "Prince Jonathan".
Prince Jonathan.
With all the noblewomen, I end up feeling like the last kernel of corn in the hen house. But you, you pit me in my place when I'm wrong..
And you are wrong pretty often.

This isn't how it's supposed to go, but... I hoped to only ask this question once in my life, but I'm going to ask it twice. Once now with this...  ring made of straw, but I will come back in two weeks and turn this ring into gold. Well, I'll bring you a gold one. And I'll whisk you away to the castle Princess Cora.  Will you marry me?
Yes. Yes!
You've made me so happy. Oh, but these next two weeks are going to be torture. Meet me at the crossroads so we don't waste any time.
I'll be there. When do you have to leave?
Very early tomorrow, I'm afraid.
The tavern closes soon.
Well, then...
But, seeing as how we're essentially married, perhaps I can keep your company in your room.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Emma Swan enters the living room. Thoughtful, Hook looks at a green apple in his hand.

dialog.dialog: I'd watch out for the apples in this house.
Aye.
It was a joke. Where's your sense of humor?
It left, when the witch arrived.
Well, we're gonna take care of that. Regina said she has a plan.
I'm sure she does.
Emma, will you please tell your mother that we're not gonna name your brother"Leopold"?
Uh, why not? It was my father's name.
People will make fun of him.
My father was a king.
Which is why, nobody made fun of him.
Okay, what about "Eva“ after my mother?
I don't know, how that'll work with a boy.
It might not be a boy.
Sorry to keep you waiting. I was making this special tea.  No, don't drink it! It's a deadly poison summoning the Dark Vortex.
I prefer Earl Grey.
The dark what?

Zelena came by this morning. While the Dark One was stealing my heart, she was here gloating. She said that my weakness was that I don't know enough of her past. So, I have decided to summon someone who does. We have to talk to my mother.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Witch Farmhouse. Carefully, Zelena takes Regina's heart out of a satchel. A flying monkey screeches.

dialog.dialog: Yes. I know, it looks battered, but that's the point.  This will do perfectly.  Oh, don't be jealous.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Witch Farmhouse. The storm cellar. Mr. Gold suspiciously eyes the black coat Zelena has handed him.

dialog.dialog: What do you want me to do with this?

What do you think? Wear it. I've got Regina's heart, Charming'scourage and right in here, ready to be plucked, your lovely brains.  We've got everything we need. It's a good time to celebrate, don't you think?
You don't have everything.
The baby will come. Now, let's get you dressed.
I'm not your doll.

Aren't you? I'm not using the dagger, but I could. A certain baby could be here at any minute, so I'm going to give you a choice on how you want to live out the rest of this time. One choice is, screaming agony. The other is much more pleasant. Choose well, doll.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House.

dialog.dialog: It's a fairly simple ritual, but not often performed.
If it's easy to talk to the dead, why don't do it more?
Well, because to do it, you need the murder weapon and the murderer.
What do we need to do?
Focus on Cora.
Welcoming thoughts?
Whatever you've got.
Is it...?
We did it.
Cora? Mother, can you hear us? Cora, give us a sign.  Do not ignore me now, mother. Please, you owe me this.
Sorry, love. That was me. I crossed my legs and bumped the table.
Do we try it again?
No, there's no point. It worked. The portal opened, but nothing came from it. She doesn't wanna talk to me. Guess, whatever secrets lie in her past, she wants to keep buried there.

scenes.sceneDesc: Enchanted Forest. Past. Enchanted Forest. Past, two months later. Cora walks down the road. It's raining. The scene shifts to a garden. Jonathan is standing inside a pavilion waiting.

dialog.dialog: Jonathan! Jonathan! Jonathan, it's me. What happened? It's been two months. My friends said it was a trick, but I said 'no, it must be an accident or a misunderstanding that I'd find out at the castle.' And here you are.
You're a stubborn one, aren't you?
Are you the gardener?
Well, I'm not Prince Jonathan. Of course, there is no Prince Jonathan.
You lied to me. You took my heart, my virtue. My good name.
I took? You practically forced it into my hands, when you thought that I was a prince. A horlot is a harlot.

I'm with child. I'm going to have a baby. There may be no Prince Jonathan, but there is a prince and how would he feel, when I tell him what you've done? Give me enough to support this child. You stole before, do it again.
Yeah, go ahead and tell him. Good luck finding me.
Guards! Guards! There's a scoundrel in the garden. Guards, help me.
Are you alright? What happened to you? You were calling for help.
He got away. Just a bandit on the road. He took everything I had.
I'm so sorry to hear that.  Come with me. We'll do everything we can for you.
There's nothing you can do for me.
Allow me to try. I'm Prince Leopold.
Thank you, your Highness.

scenes.sceneDesc: Present day. Storybrooke. Mills house. David walks out of the living room. Emma and Hook follow him.

dialog.dialog: Maybe Belle will have more luck. There has to be something that can help us.
Right, my magic gets more powerful every day. By the time, this all goes down, I'll be ready.
Make sure you are.
Come on, let's go.
You know what? I'm gonna stay and talk to Regina. I'll help clean up.
I'm not in the mood for a heart-to-heart.
I'm not sure that's physically possible right now.
You can help with the tea cups.

scenes.sceneDesc: Enchanted Forest. Past. Cora and Prince Leopold are walking together in the woods.

dialog.dialog: There it was: My oldest night dress. The boys tied it to the top of the windmill, flapping in the wind for the whole village to see.  And I'm the maddest eight-year-old you ever saw.
Did you ever get revenge for that?
That's a long term plan. You see, there aren't that many young people in that village and, odds are, one of them will end up married to me. That should do it.
You're wonderful.  And cold.
It's a bit frosty, but I'm okay.
Uh, I'd build a fire for you, but I'm embarrassed to say, I don't know how.
I do.
I'm going to be king.
I gather that happens, when you're a prince.
And I wanna do right by that gift. I keep telling myself I wanna be a man of the people, and then I realize I can't even build a fire. A task every peasant can do.
Well, this peasant can fix that.  See, just have to let air get in.
When the throne becomes mine, I'll need to provide an heir of my own.
You'll have to marry.
And start a family immediately.
That actually sounds nice. Anyone in mind?
Yes.  Princess Eva from the northern kingdom. We've been engaged since her birth.
Oh, is she nice?
I'll let you know after I meet her. That's tonight.
You don't sound very excited. I'm sure she's... a lovely choice.
I'm sure she is. She's just not a choice I made.
What's the use of having power if you can't choose who to wed?

scenes.sceneDesc: Present day. Mills House. The kitchen. Regina has her back turned to Mary Margaret, who's sitting on the table.

dialog.dialog: I wanna apologize, Regina. For Cora. For...
murdering her?
Right. Yeah. I don't take it lightly. I think about it every day.
So do I.  And when I do think about it, I remember that she did kill your mother.  So, I'll admit that it's complicated.
Thank you. I'm sure you had some things you wanted to say to her.

Yes. Well, I'm realizing that my mother walled off a lot of her life from me. She wasn't the warmest mother, but at least I thought I occupied a singular spot in her heat.  A sister. Zelena. Why would she give her up? Why would she make us strangers to one another?
What's that?
I have no idea.
It's upstairs.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It's in there.  There isn't anything in this room. It's...  Mother?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Granny's diner. Emma is trying to do magic.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (she succeeds; moving the cup with hot coca to where Kilian was sitting at the table). Boom! Granny's to go. I should open a franchise.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It's impressive.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You wanna see something really impressive? (Killian sighs, as Emma puts his hook on the hanger. She chuckles)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It's bad form, Swan, tampering with a man's hook.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (Chuckling) Okay, seriously, what is up with you?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I apologise for my rudeness. It's a long story, too long for now.(He takes a sip of his drink)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Okay. Well, obviously something's…(She's interrupted by Belle rushing into the diner)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Emma, I f- I found it. We have to tell Regina and the others.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Okay, Belle, slow down, what did you find?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Zelena's plan. I figured out what she's doing. (Emma looks at her with a shocked expression)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's house

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You should have a woman dress you more often.(She gets closer to MrGold) Please, do have a seat.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (Moving towards the table) I wonder how much civility I'll be asked to endure before this evening's true purpose is revealed.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (Chuckles) Relax. You know I'm not one for subtlety.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, how lucky for me.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Play coy if you like (pours him a glass of wine). But we both know it wasn't a dagger that brought you into my kitchen.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well , coercion take many forms, my dear, but I can assure you, I'm not here for the boiled peas.(timer dings)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: No, you're here for my secret, aren't you? Hmm? Why a heart? Why courage? Why a brain? Why this particular baby?(smiling)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: And more to the point, why is it of any interest to me?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (she puts the food on the table) Because, my secret will reunite you with your long lost son.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My son is not lost. He's dead. You watched him die.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: This isn't idle cruelty. I promise you, he's alive and waiting for you to find him.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Find him where?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Not where, when. I'm going to change the past.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (almost laughing to her face) No. Time travel spells have been written since the dawn of the dark arts but never cast. It's against the fundamental laws of magic.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (Chuckles) Magic doesn't fail. People fail. Laws only exist until they're broken by someone…superior.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You.(Zelena chuckles) You've deciphered the laws of time travel?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: When that baby is born, I am going back to claim the life that I deserve, and there's no reason that you can't come with me.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: To find my boy. Perhaps never give him up.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills house. Upstairs

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: This can't be. The spell opened a limited portal for…talking.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It's me. I killed Cora. She sensed my presence and found a way to bleed through into our world. (Cora tries to get to Mary Margaret, but Regina steps in front of her)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Watch out. Back down mother. You will answer my questions. Now, tell me what did you do to Zelena?(Cora pushes Regina away. She tries to get to Mary Margaret again and Regina whooshes them downstairs. Cora appears through the wall, but Regina tries to hold her off with her magic)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You're holding her off.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: But I can't hold her forever.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Enchanted Forest. Past. Cora is outside, in the garden, admiring her engagement ring.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, low and be hold. It turned into gold after all.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: How dare you. You must have a brain the size of a pixie to show your face in this kingdom.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, I had to see for myself if the commoner who said yes to Prince Leopold was the same who said it to me…several times…in one night. That's quite impressive.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: In two days, I will be princess and you will bow down and count yourself lucky to pull the weeds at my feet.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Ohh! Been practicing in front of the mirror, eh? You're as much of a fraud as I ever was.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My love for Prince Leopold is true, as is his for me.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: But does he know how much of you there is to love? Or were you hoping to keep our baby a secret?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What is it you want? – Gold? Jewels?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Mmm, yes. Whatever you can get. Enough for me to live happily for the rest of my days, just like you wanted for me. Bring it, here tomorrow night and your secret shall remain just that.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It will be done. Now leave before someone sees you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (Princess Eva is listening to their conversation)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Princess Eva, your carriage awaits. Princess Eva?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I'm coming.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills' house

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I don't know how much longer I can hold her off.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (gasps) Cora, I'm sorry. Please forgive me! (gasps)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Save your breath. Your mother was nothing like my mother. She only understands power and weakness. But if she wants to kill you, she's gonna have to go through me! This isn't about you. This is about her and me(Regina stops using her powers) It's time for answers mother. What did you do to Zelena?(Cora knocks her down and possesses Mary Margaret)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (grunts) No! (falls onto a chair. Cora shows her a vision)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Enchanted Forest. Past. Prince Leopold's castle

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My love, you've frightened me.(she kisses him)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What are you doing in the great hall at this hour?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, a wedding won't plan itself, and there's endless amounts of work to me done. Is something wrong?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Word has spread throughout the castle that you are with child.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My love, I am so sorry for the terrible trouble that this has brought upon you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Gossip means nothing to me. I've pledged ro marry you, dear Cora, and I will keep that pledge as long as I know I can trust you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (sighs) There is no baby. Only my love for you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You only love yourself.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: : What is she doing here?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Princess Eva is here because she overheard your rendezvous in the garden yesterday.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: And just as I said, the miller's daughter is a worhless liar.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You awful child. Yes, I was in the garden yesterday. There was a man roaming the grounds. I feared he might steal something so I made hm leave. Whatever else Eva has told you is the invention of a gelous brat who would say anything to marry you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Check her pockets.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: No.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: There's nothing left to say

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You deserve better that her lies

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (exhales sharply) Leopold! Don't let me go!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Come, Eva. Help me break this terrible news to the king.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Today your heart aches, your highness, but I promise a more worthy wife will give you the child that you deserve. One that will be pure, pure as

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills' house

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Mary Margaret! Hold on. I won't let you go(Regina pulls Cora out of Mary Margaret's body with her magic and sends her away).

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (coming in) What the hell was that?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Cora. Is she okay?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Mary Margaret, can you hear me?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: (weakly)Cora…not what we thought. She's trying to communicate.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: She was? What'd she say?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Leopold, my mother, my mother…

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Shhh. Mary Margaret,relax.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: She's not making any sense. We need to call a doctor.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: If she knows something that could help us-

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Regina it's gonna have to wait.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It can't wait! If we wanna stop Zelena, we have to know what the hell she's doing and why!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I can help with half of that.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: She's planning on going back. Back in time.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Are you certain?(Belle nods) No one's ever been able to cast a spell to go back in time.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well clearly she thinks she can succeed. I mean brains, courage, a-a resilient heart. Those ingredients are in every time-travel spell I've found.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Why go back in time, I mean we have no idea what she's trying to accomplish.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I do. She-she didn't…

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Don't push yourself.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: She didn't want to give up Zelena. She was forced to by my mm-by my mother. Princess Eva, she told a secret. Just like I did.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Wait. I thought our family were the good guys.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Life is too messy for it to ever be that simple.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You're saying if it wasn't for Snow's mother, Cora would have never gave up Zelena.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: She would have been tutored by Rumplestilskin

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: All if it weren't for this Princess Eva?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: So that means…

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Zelena's going back to kill my mother.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: [Gasps] You'll have never been born.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I'm guessing this is where your help ends.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Now, think it through, Ms. swan. That means you'll have never been born, and neither will Henry.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: And on this different path, Regina, you may not be, either.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It's a good thing no one has ever succeeded with this time-travel nonsense.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: The baby.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: That's what's missing. That's why none have succeeded. Somehow, some way, our baby is the key. Zelena went to a lot of effort to get close to our unborn child. That's what she's after.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What is she going to do to with? –

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: It doesn't matter. She's not gonna get it. We're gonna stop her. Since our baby's not born, she's stuck, and we have what we need Time.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Just not very much of it.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: In a little cottage. Past [Screams] [Panting]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: One more. Big one. (Cora grunts)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Push! (Cora Groans)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE:In the woods. Past.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Poor babe. (the baby coos) (Voice breaking) Life is cruel and full of betrayal. That is my only lesson for you. Now I must give you away (baby coos) To give me My best chance. As long as I have you, I can never be anything more than a Miller's daughter. (The baby strats crying) [Wind gusting]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's house.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Thank you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: For what?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: For showing me something I should have seen long ago. It's never easy for a man to admit he's wrong. But I wronged you and hurt us both.(he pours wine in the glasses)

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Those mistakes can't haunt us. Not anymore. Soon, history will be wiped clean. [Chuckles]
Until then, a gentleman always makes amends for his failings. [Exhales sharply] [Breathing heavily] [Moaning] [Gasps] [Whoosh] [Panting]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Sorry, love. Afraid not!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Never on the first date, eh, dearie?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: You just wanted the knife. [Exhales sharply] I hope you enjoyed the taste, because you just squandered your only chance to have your son!

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My son gave his life so I could stop you, and I can see only one way to honor that sacrifice, and that's by killing you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Celebration's over. Back to your cage Doll. [Door opens] [Breathing heavily] [Sighs]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills' house.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Need a hand?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Shouldn't you be on bed rest?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Dr. Whale just left. He cleared me and the baby. Apparently, the human body has a surprising tolerance for ghost possession. And he told me I have a tough placenta, which somehow came out creepy. May I? You want to talk about it?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: What's there to talk about? You officially have a lessdamaging relationship with my mother than I do. And you killed her.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, at least we know, you and me, our history. It's more complicated than we thought.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I know how much you looked up to Eva. I'm sorry you had to learn she had such darkness in her past.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: That wasn't the woman I knew. It makes me wonder what happened that changed her. I suppose I'll never know.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, we can never know our past completely. If we had, I probably wouldn't have spent so much time trying to kill you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Well, we would've found something to fight about. I mean I was such a brat.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Your mother's child. [Chuckles lightly]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: I think we've wasted our last day being haunted by the past.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Now we can focus on being haunted by our impending lack of a future.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Zelena isn't going to win. Not with all of us united against her.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Hope isn't easy when I know she holds my heart.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: Which you'll get back, stronger than ever. That's what it means to be the most resilient. That could be another one of her tricks, too. Regina, I've seen what life has thrown at you. And you still fight against the darkness every single day. Sooner or later, your heart will find its way to happiness.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: That doesn't feel possible.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: But it is. I know you, and you feel things deeply. With or without it, you feel things with your whole soul. Don't let anything hold you back. [Sighs]

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: SCENE: Storybrooke. Present day. Regina is walking to Robin's camp site.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills House. Upstairs, Regina and Mary Margaret carefully approach a door.

dialog.dialog: My lady. I'm sorry. I let you down. Your heart was lost to Zelena on my watch, but I promise you, I will get it back. (They look into each other's eyes, Regina pulls him in and kisses him.)
